architecture_fit.note: Single attention head, MLP dropped — minimal delta from base_model; logits = -beta*f solve the goal directly (canonical alignment 0.86).

baseline_comparison.note: Clean g+h vs h-only vs g-only vs uniform ablation: full top1=0.76/align=0.86 while strawmen collapse (uniform 0.0, h-only top1 0.0).

faithfulness.note: Synthetic circuit (no model) is stated; component knock-outs of g and h stand in for the ablation, though no model-level patching check is proposed.

operating_range.note: Density sweep 0.0–0.4 holds (alignment 0.87→0.68, robustness 0.79) and degrades visibly at 0.4; single grid size, one axis only.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-built: f=g+h computed by hand (Manhattan h, BFS min-plus g) with no training — full mechanism written out.

visual_judgement.note: Demo pairs attention vs A* f-value heatmaps with agent/goal/optimal-next/argmax overlays plus an ablation bar chart — claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties side-by-side heatmaps to the low-f claim and the bar chart to the 'aligns where strawmen don't' baseline argument.

notes: A hand-built single-head A* attention circuit that cleanly solves the goal: attention mass tracks low-f cells (canonical alignment 0.86, top3 1.0) and the argmax lands on the optimal next neighbour. The g/h component ablation provides a strong baseline-and-faithfulness story for a synthetic attempt, and the visualisation directly checks the claim. Loses some ground on model-level causal evidence and breadth of operating range, but is a solid, well-justified result.
